2603.22968.title: Beyond Theoretical Bounds: Empirical Privacy Loss Calibration for Text Rewriting Under Local Differential Privacy
2603.22968.paper_summary: The growing use of large language models has increased interest in sharing textual data in a privacy-preserving manner. One prominent line of work addresses this challenge through text rewriting under Local Differential Privacy (LDP), where input texts are locally obfuscated before release with formal privacy guarantees. These guarantees are typically expressed by a parameter $\varepsilon$ that upper bounds the worst-case privacy loss. However, nominal $\varepsilon$ values are often difficult to interpret and compare across mechanisms. In this work, we investigate how to empirically calibrate across text rewriting mechanisms under LDP. We propose TeDA, which formulates calibration via a hypothesis-testing framework that instantiates text distinguishability audits in both surface and embedding spaces, enabling empirical assessment of indistinguishability from privatized texts. Applying this calibration to several representative mechanisms, we demonstrate that similar nominal $\varepsilon$ bounds can imply very different levels of distinguishability. Empirical calibration thus provides a more comparable footing for evaluating privacy-utility trade-offs, as well as a practical tool for mechanism comparison and analysis in real-world LDP text rewriting deployments.
2603.22968.paper_summary_zh: 隨著大型語言模型的日益使用，對於以保護隱私的方式共享文本數據的興趣也在增加。<lb/>一個突出的研究方向通過在本地差分隱私（LDP）下進行文本重寫來應對這一挑戰，其中輸入文本在釋放之前會進行本地混淆，並提供正式的隱私保證。<lb/>這些保證通常由一個參數 $\varepsilon$ 表達，該參數上限了最壞情況下的隱私損失。<lb/>然而，名義上的 $\varepsilon$ 值往往難以解釋，並且在不同機制之間進行比較也很困難。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們研究如何在 LDP 下對文本重寫機制進行實證校準。<lb/>我們提出了 TeDA，通過假設檢驗框架來制定校準，該框架在表面和嵌入空間中實現文本可區分性審計，使得能夠對私有化文本的不可區分性進行實證評估。<lb/>將這一校準應用於幾個代表性機制，我們證明了相似的名義 $\varepsilon$ 上限可能暗示著非常不同的可區分性水平。<lb/>因此，實證校準為評估隱私與效用的權衡提供了更可比較的基礎，並且為現實世界的 LDP 文本重寫部署中的機制比較和分析提供了一個實用工具。
2603.22968.author: Weijun Li et.al.
2603.22968.authors: Weijun Li, Arnaud Grivet Sébert, Qiongkai Xu, Annabelle McIver, Mark Dras
